channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: States threaten fines and jail time for local officials who resist Trump's immigration crackdown
item.description: Republican lawmakers in some states are threatening local officials with lawsuits, fines and jail time if they resist President Donald Trump's crackdown on illegal immigration.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Kramon And David A. Lieb, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Musk gives all federal workers 48 hours to explain what they did last week
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Hundreds of thousands of federal workers have been given little more than 48 hours to explain what they accomplished over the last week, sparking confusion across key agencies as billionaire Elon Musk expands his crusade to slash the size of federal government.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New FBI leader Kash Patel tapped to run ATF as acting director
item.description: Newly-sworn in FBI Director Kash Patel will be tapped to run the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives as acting director, according to sources.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal prisons prep to move trans inmates as early as next week
item.description: The Bureau of Prisons is moving forward with plans to move transgender inmates out of prisons that align with their gender identity and into facilities that align with their assigned sex at birth.
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Diaz, MPR News

item.title: Watch live: Trump set to address CPAC
item.description: President Trump on Saturday afternoon will headline the final day of the Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) in Maryland.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump revels in mass federal firings and jeers at Biden before adoring conservative crowd
item.description: President Donald Trump says “nobody has ever seen anything” like his administration’s sweeping effort to fire thousands of federal employees and shrink the size of government.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert And Adriana Gomez Licon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Many Americans don't trust the media to cover Trump: Survey
item.description: Most Americans in a new survey said they have little confidence that the news media can cover President Trump with fairness and accuracy.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: A look at Dan 'Razin' Caine, Trump's pick to be the top US military officer
item.description: President Donald Trump’s choice to be the top U.S. military officer, retired Air Force Lt. Gen. Dan Caine, endeared himself to the commander in chief through his military call sign, “Razin,” during a meeting in Iraq years ago.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: At CPAC, Argentina’s Milei explains his chainsaw methods and likens them to Musk’s DOGE
item.description: OXON HILL, Md. (AP) — Argentina’s President Javier Milei on Saturday urged an enthusiastic crowd of U.S. conservatives to keep supporting measures to shrink the size of their government, saying his methods are similar to those undertaken by President Donald Trump and close ally Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: CPAC straw poll reveals who conservatives believe will be 2028 presidential nominee
item.description: Vice President JD Vance will be the GOP's presidential nominee in the 2028 election cycle, a straw poll conducted at the 2025 Conservative Political Action Conference found.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Meet four Minnesota federal workers mourning the loss of their dream jobs
item.description: Many Minnesotans are among those facing unemployment, as the Trump administration slashes jobs across agencies. The state is home to more than 18,000 federal workers, with nearly 60 percent working for the Department of Veterans Affairs and the U.S. Department of Agriculture.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: The Memo: Trump’s jabs at Ukraine divide GOP
item.description: President Trump ratcheted up his attacks on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Friday, deepening the first real schism of his second term within the GOP.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Michigan state lawmaker beats out loyal Trump ally charged in fake elector case for GOP party chair
item.description: Michigan Republicans have elected a state lawmaker to lead the GOP into the 2026 midterms, State Sen.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Volmert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: White House and Ukraine nearing rare earths deal that would tighten relationship, AP source says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The White House and Ukraine have made significant progress toward reaching an agreement that would provide the U.S. with access to Ukraine's rare earth minerals and tighten the long-term relationship between Kyiv and Washington, according to a person familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: U.S. Department of Agriculture suspends scholarship program at NC A&T State University, 18 other historically Black colleges, website shows
item.description: GREENSBORO, N.C. (WGHP) — The U.S. Department of Agriculture has suspended a scholarship program that serves students from rural or underserved communities pending further review, according to its website.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Ratliff, NewsNation

item.title: Judge clears way for Trump administration to pull thousands of USAID staffers off the job
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Friday cleared the way for one of the Trump administration's remaining steps in its dismantling of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Justice Department files complaint against judge weighing challenge to Trump’s transgender troop ban
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department filed a complaint Friday accusing a federal judge in Washington of misconduct during hearings over President Donald Trump's executive order that calls for banning transgender troops from serving in the U.S. military.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 takeaways from Elon Musk's CPAC talk
item.description: Elon Musk served as one of the main attractions at the Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) with a late-scheduled appearance that saw him wield a chainsaw.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Justice Department broadens Jan. 6 pardons to cover gun, drug-related charges
item.description: In a new development, the Department of Justice said Trump's pardon of Jan. 6 defendants should apply more broadly and include separate gun charges, as well.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dreisbach, MPR News

item.title: Planned medical research grant cuts worry Sarasota scientists
item.description: For more than 20 years, Sarasota-based biomedical research facility The Roskamp Institute has been focused on understanding and finding treatment, prevention, and early detection for Alzheimer's disease and other dementia, along with illnesses facing veterans.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bill that would make more attorney general data public fails in Minnesota House vote
item.description: House Republicans pushed for a vote on a bill to require the attorney general’s office, occupied now by DFLer Keith Ellison, to be more forthcoming with records. Democrats framed it as a partisan measure.
item.dc:creator: Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: Ellison: Trump order on transgender athletes violates Minnesota law
item.description: Attorney General Keith Ellison’s opinion comes days after the Trump administration said it would investigate the state high school league’s plan to follow state law and let students participate in sports consistent with their gender identity, despite the president’s executive order.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Shockman and Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Cortez Masto, Rosen introduce proposal to block Trump plan to end birthright citizenship
item.description: Nevada Democratic Sens. Catherine Cortez Masto and Jacky Rosen introduced a proposal Thursday to block President Donald Trump's attempt to end the constitutional right to birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate confirms Kash Patel to lead FBI
item.description: The Senate on Thursday confirmed Kash Patel to serve as the head of the FBI, approving a pick with a string of controversial statements who has been accused by Democrats of directing a purge of bureau employees. Patel was confirmed in a 51-49 vote. Two Republicans, Sens.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Frey vetoes council move toward a pedestrian plaza at George Floyd Square
item.description: Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey has vetoed the City Council’s plan to explore building a pedestrian-only mall at George Floyd Square, the intersection where an officer killed Floyd in 2020.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Trump approval rating appears to be underwater one month in: Survey
item.description: The CNN poll shows that while 47 percent of adults in the U.S. say they approve of Trump's moves since returning to the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Senate GOP dismayed by Trump, Zelensky war of words
item.description: Public comments from President Trump blaming Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky for Russia’s invasion of his country are unnerving Senate Republicans, who have largely sought to avoid conflicts with the White House.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Latino business owners say customers are staying away in wake of deportation threats
item.description: Some Latino business owners say customers have been staying away out of fear of being detained and deported. Twin Cities business and city leaders are encouraging people to buy from Latino-owned shops this Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Regina Medina, MPR News

item.title: Florida GOP pushes for government efficiency with DOGE-inspired legislation
item.description: President Donald Trump's Department of Government Efficiency, also known as DOGE, has stirred up controversy as it searches for wasteful federal spending. Now, Florida GOP lawmakers are planning a similar initiative.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DOGE released data about federal contract savings. It doesn’t add up
item.description: A new government tracker claims DOGE has saved billions from ending federal contracts. But an NPR analysis of the data finds the claimed savings don’t add up.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler, MPR News

item.title: Florida Democrats file bill to repeal 6-week abortion ban
item.description: House Minority Leader Fentrice Driskell, who represents families in Hillsborough County, said the legislation comes because it’s a mandate from voters.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New York rep. says Trump has committed 'dozens of impeachable offenses' so far
item.description: “Donald Trump has, in his first month, probably committed dozens of impeachable offenses,” Rep. Dan Goldman (D-N.Y.) said in a recent interview.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Minnesota bill seeks to disable AI tech used to create nude or sexual images of people
item.description: The plan would require artificial intelligence companies to deactivate a feature that allows users to make people appear naked or engaged in sexual acts in photos or videos without their consent.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, Nina Moini, and Alanna Elder, MPR News

item.title: Nevada may expand revenge porn law to cover AI
item.description: A proposal at the Nevada Legislature would amend the state’s revenge porn law to include computer-generated images.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge refuses to block DOGE’s access to student borrower data
item.description: A judge is also set to rule soon on whether to block DOGE from accessing systems at seven federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Social Security head steps down over DOGE data access: Sources
item.description: King's exit from the administration is one of several departures of high-ranking officials concerned about DOGE staffers' potential unlawful access to private taxpayer information.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
